description: Selank is a synthetic heptapeptide analog of the endogenous immunomodulatory tetrapeptide tuftsin, studied in preclinical models for its anxiolytic and nootropic properties through modulation of GABAergic tone and BDNF expression in limbic structures.

researchLinks.title: Selank stabilizes the expression of BDNF and its precursor in rat brain structures under stress
Selank and short tuftsin fragments influence on enkephalin levels in mice
Pharmacological properties of the peptide anxiolytic selank at different routes of administration

description: Semax is a synthetic heptapeptide derivative of ACTH(4–10) investigated in preclinical and early clinical research for its neuroprotective and cognitive-enhancing effects, including upregulation of BDNF and NGF in hippocampal and cortical tissue.

researchLinks.title: Semax affects the expression of genes related to the inflammatory response in rats with ischemic brain injury
Neuroprotective and nootropic drug Semax affects the expression of genes related to the dopaminergic system
Semax, an analog of ACTH(4-7), regulates expression of BDNF and its receptor (TrkB) mRNA in the rat brain

description: Melanotan I (afamelanotide) is a synthetic linear analog of alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α-MSH) that binds melanocortin receptors with high affinity and is studied for its role in melanogenesis and photoprotective pigmentation responses in preclinical models.

researchLinks.title: Photosensitivity, tanning, and melanocyte-stimulating hormone analogs
Analog of alpha-MSH with superpotent and prolonged photoprotective effects on human skin in vivo
Afamelanotide and narrowband UV-B phototherapy for the treatment of vitiligo

description: Melanotan II is a cyclic lactam analog of α-MSH with broad melanocortin receptor binding activity, studied in preclinical models for its potent effects on pigmentation signaling and melanocyte activation pathways.

researchLinks.title: Structure-activity studies of Melanotan II, a superpotent cyclic melanotropic peptide
Melanocortin receptors and erectile function
PT-141: a melanocortin agonist for the treatment of sexual dysfunction

description: MT-1 (Melanotan I / afamelanotide) is a linear α-MSH analog that activates MC1R on melanocytes, examined in research settings for its role in eumelanin synthesis induction and UV-independent pigmentation signaling.

researchLinks.title: Photosensitivity, tanning, and melanocyte-stimulating hormone analogs
Analog of alpha-MSH with superpotent and prolonged photoprotective effects on human skin in vivo
Afamelanotide for erythropoietic protoporphyria

description: MT-2 (Melanotan II) is a cyclic heptapeptide α-MSH analog studied in preclinical models for non-selective melanocortin receptor activation, melanogenesis, and downstream signaling through the MC1R and MC4R subtypes.

researchLinks.title: Structure-activity studies of Melanotan II, a superpotent cyclic melanotropic peptide
Melanocortin receptors and erectile function
PT-141: a melanocortin agonist for the treatment of sexual dysfunction

description: IGF-1 LR3 (Long R3 Insulin-like Growth Factor-1) is an 83-amino acid analog of endogenous IGF-1 with an N-terminal extension and Arg3 substitution that reduces IGFBP binding affinity, conferring an extended half-life studied in in vitro and in vivo models of growth factor signaling.

researchLinks.title: Insulin-like growth factor-I receptor signalling and its extension: a further look at the multiple mechanisms
Characterization of a Long R3 IGF-I with high receptor binding and IGF-binding protein affinity
IGF-1 and its binding proteins: structural and functional aspects

description: This blend combines CJC-1295 without DAC (a GHRH analog with a short pulsatile action profile) and Ipamorelin (a selective GHS-R agonist), studied in preclinical models for their synergistic effects on pulsatile GH secretion from pituitary somatotrophs.

researchLinks.title: CJC-1295, a long-acting growth hormone-releasing hormone analog, increases GH and IGF-1 in healthy adults
Ipamorelin, a novel growth hormone secretagogue, selectively stimulates growth hormone secretion
Ipamorelin: a new growth-hormone-releasing peptide

description: Ipamorelin is a selective, pentapeptide GH secretagogue that binds the GHS-R1a receptor and is studied in preclinical models for its ability to stimulate pulsatile GH release with high specificity and without significant co-stimulation of ACTH or cortisol pathways.

researchLinks.title: Ipamorelin, a new growth-hormone-releasing peptide, selectively stimulates growth hormone secretion
In vivo and in vitro effects of a new growth hormone-releasing peptide, ipamorelin
Ipamorelin increases bone mass and affects bone mineral density in ovariectomized rats

description: Seramorelin is a synthetic 29-amino acid peptide corresponding to the biologically active N-terminal fragment of endogenous growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH), studied for its pituitary GHRH receptor binding and pulsatile GH release induction in preclinical models.

researchLinks.title: Synthetic growth hormone-releasing peptides: a review
Effects of growth hormone-releasing hormone on sleep and growth hormone secretion in elderly men
Growth hormone-releasing hormone: biological mechanisms

description: Tesamorelin is a stabilized synthetic analog of GHRH(1–44) in which the N-terminal region is conjugated to a trans-3-hexenoic acid moiety to confer resistance to enzymatic cleavage, studied in controlled trials for its effects on GH/IGF-1 axis modulation and visceral adipose tissue metabolism.

researchLinks.title: Tesamorelin, a synthetic human growth hormone-releasing factor, reduces abdominal fat in HIV-infected patients
Effects of tesamorelin on visceral fat and liver fat in HIV-infected patients with abdominal fat accumulation
Long-term effects of tesamorelin on visceral fat and metabolic parameters in HIV-infected patients

description: BPC-157 (Body Protection Compound-157) is a synthetic pentadecapeptide derived from a sequence found in human gastric juice, extensively studied in preclinical models for its cytoprotective, angiogenic, and tissue-repair-promoting activities across musculoskeletal and gastrointestinal systems.

researchLinks.title: Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157: novel therapy in gastrointestinal tract
BPC 157, a pentadecapeptide, stimulates angiogenesis and accelerates healing in preclinical models
Cytoprotective effect of BPC 157 on the gastric mucosa in vivo

description: GHK-Cu (glycine-histidine-lysine copper(II)) is a naturally occurring tripeptide-copper complex found in human plasma, studied extensively for its regulatory effects on collagen synthesis, antioxidant gene expression, and tissue remodeling signaling in in vitro and in vivo models.

researchLinks.title: The human tripeptide GHK-Cu in prevention of oxidative stress and degenerative conditions of aging: implications for cognitive health
GHK and DNA: resetting the human genome to health
The effect of the human peptide GHK on gene expression relevant to nervous system function and cognitive decline

description: GHK-Cu (glycine-histidine-lysine copper(II)) is a naturally occurring tripeptide-copper complex found in human plasma, studied extensively for its regulatory effects on collagen synthesis, antioxidant gene expression, and tissue remodeling signaling in in vitro and in vivo models.

researchLinks.title: The human tripeptide GHK-Cu in prevention of oxidative stress and degenerative conditions of aging: implications for cognitive health

description: Glow is a research blend of GHK-Cu (50mg), BPC-157 (10mg), and Thymosin Beta-4 (10mg), combining a copper tripeptide studied for extracellular matrix remodeling with a cytoprotective pentadecapeptide and an actin-sequestering peptide examined individually for their complementary roles in tissue repair and angiogenic signaling.

researchLinks.title: The human tripeptide GHK-Cu in prevention of oxidative stress and degenerative conditions of aging

description: Glutathione (γ-L-glutamyl-L-cysteinyl-glycine) is a ubiquitous intracellular tripeptide antioxidant studied extensively in biochemistry for its central role in redox homeostasis, xenobiotic detoxification, and maintenance of thiol-disulfide balance across cell types.

researchLinks.title: Glutathione and its role in cellular functions
Glutathione: overview of its protective roles, measurement, and biosynthesis
Glutathione and oxidative stress in preclinical models of aging and disease

description: Klow is a research blend of GHK-Cu (50mg), BPC-157 (10mg), Thymosin Beta-4 (10mg), and KPV (10mg), extending the Glow formulation with the addition of the alpha-MSH-derived anti-inflammatory tripeptide KPV, which is studied for its NF-κB inhibition and mucosal cytoprotective signaling in preclinical inflammation models.

researchLinks.title: The human tripeptide GHK-Cu in prevention of oxidative stress and degenerative conditions of aging
Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157: novel therapy in gastrointestinal tract

description: KPV (Lys-Pro-Val) is the biologically active C-terminal tripeptide of alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone, studied in preclinical models for its direct anti-inflammatory activity through inhibition of NF-κB signaling in intestinal epithelial cells and macrophages.

researchLinks.title: Alpha-MSH C-terminal tripeptide KPV inhibits NF-κB activation and reduces intestinal inflammation

description: Thymosin Beta-4 (TB-500) is a 43-amino acid water-soluble peptide originally isolated from thymic tissue, studied extensively in preclinical models for its G-actin sequestration properties, promotion of angiogenesis, and modulation of wound healing and inflammatory signaling.

researchLinks.title: Thymosin β4 and its role in wound healing and tissue repair

description: Wolverine is a research blend combining BPC-157 and Thymosin Beta-4 (TB-500), two peptides independently studied for their complementary roles in cytoprotection, angiogenesis, actin dynamics, and multi-tissue repair signaling in preclinical musculoskeletal and gastrointestinal models.

researchLinks.title: Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157: novel therapy in gastrointestinal tract
Thymosin β4 and its role in wound healing and tissue repair
BPC 157 and TB-4 synergistic effects on tendon repair in preclinical models

description: Wolverine is a research blend combining BPC-157 and Thymosin Beta-4 (TB-500), two peptides independently studied for their complementary roles in cytoprotection, angiogenesis, actin dynamics, and multi-tissue repair signaling in preclinical musculoskeletal and gastrointestinal models.

researchLinks.title: Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157: novel therapy in gastrointestinal tract
Thymosin β4 and its role in wound healing and tissue repair

description: Vasoactive Intestinal Peptide (VIP) is a 28-amino acid neuropeptide belonging to the glucagon/secretin superfamily, studied in preclinical models for its immunomodulatory effects including suppression of pro-inflammatory cytokine production and promotion of regulatory T-cell differentiation.

researchLinks.title: Vasoactive intestinal peptide and pituitary adenylate cyclase-activating polypeptide modulate immune function

description: Dihexa is a small, orally active peptide derived from angiotensin IV that acts as a potent HGF/c-Met system agonist, studied in preclinical models for its synaptogenic properties, hippocampal dendrite arborization promotion, and cognitive performance outcomes in aging and injury paradigms.

researchLinks.title: Dihexa, a novel HGF/c-Met agonist, facilitates synaptogenesis and improves cognitive function

description: Epithalon (Epitalon) is a synthetic tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) studied in cell culture and animal models for its reported activation of telomerase and elongation of telomeres in somatic cells, as well as modulation of neuroendocrine and circadian regulation pathways.

researchLinks.title: Epithalon activates telomerase and extends telomere length in human somatic cells
Geroprotective effect of Epithalon in aging rodents: mortality and neuroendocrine modulation

description: Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NAD+) is an essential pyridine nucleotide coenzyme studied in aging biology for its central roles in mitochondrial energy metabolism, sirtuin deacylase activity, PARP-mediated DNA repair, and the decline in tissue NAD+ availability observed in aging preclinical models.

researchLinks.title: Declining NAD+ induces a pseudohypoxic state disrupting nuclear-mitochondrial communication during aging

description: 5-Amino-1MQ (5-amino-1-methylquinolinium) is a cell-permeable small molecule inhibitor of nicotinamide N-methyltransferase (NNMT), studied in adipocyte and metabolic preclinical models for its effects on intracellular NAD+ precursor availability and lipid metabolism gene expression.

researchLinks.title: A small molecule inhibitor of NNMT that reduces adipogenesis and lipid accumulation in cultured adipocytes

description: MOTS-c is a 16-amino acid mitochondria-derived peptide (MDP) encoded within the 12S rRNA gene of the mitochondrial genome, studied in preclinical models for its translocation to the nucleus under metabolic stress and regulation of AMPK-dependent glucose metabolism and insulin sensitivity pathways.

description: SS31 (elamipretide; D-Arg-2′6′-Dmt-Lys-Phe-NH2) is a cell-permeable, mitochondria-targeted tetrapeptide that selectively concentrates at the inner mitochondrial membrane and is studied in preclinical models for its cardiolipin-stabilizing, ROS-scavenging, and ATP synthesis-enhancing properties.

description: Oxytocin is a 9-amino acid neuropeptide synthesized in the hypothalamus and studied extensively in behavioral neuroscience for its roles in social bonding, affiliative behavior, fear conditioning, and modulation of amygdala reactivity in both rodent and non-human primate preclinical models.

description: Delta Sleep-Inducing Peptide (DSIP) is an endogenous nonapeptide first isolated from rabbit cerebral venous blood, studied in preclinical neuroscience models for its sleep-modulating properties, including promotion of delta-wave (slow-wave) sleep and its potential interactions with stress hormone axes.

description: PT-141 (bremelanotide) is a cyclic heptapeptide melanocortin receptor agonist derived from Melanotan II, studied for its central nervous system activity at MC3R and MC4R and its downstream effects on neural pathways associated with arousal-related signaling in preclinical and early clinical models.

description: Cagrilintide is a long-acting fatty acid-conjugated amylin analog designed for sustained amylin receptor agonism, studied in combination with semaglutide in phase 2 and 3 trials for its additive effects on satiety signaling, gastric emptying delay, and body weight regulation in metabolic research models.

description: Bacteriostatic Water (BAC Water) is a sterile water preparation containing 0.9% benzyl alcohol as a bacteriostatic preservative, used as a standard reconstitution solvent for lyophilized peptide research compounds to maintain sterility across multiple-use vials.
